business_domains.description: Captures and manages personal identity, address, and banking data entered by operators in the Allegro Swing desktop form.
Client-side search of a mock person registry in the Vue.js web client enabling case-insensitive filtering by name, address and house number fields.
Selection and transmission of Zahlungsempfaenger (payment recipient) records containing IBAN, BIC, and validity date alongside the matched person record.
Pub/sub broadcast relay enabling the Vue.js web client to push person and payment data as JSON messages to all connected Allegro desktop subscribers via the Node.js server.

workflows.name: Desktop Form Submission
workflows.description: The operator fills in the 13-field personal data form in the Java Swing desktop client and clicks 'Anordnen' to submit the data to the HTTP backend, receive a response, and reset the form.
workflows.trigger: User clicks the 'Anordnen' (Arrange/Order) button in PocView

steps.action: Operator fills in one or more form fields (Vorname, Name, Geburtsdatum, Geschlecht, Strasse, PLZ, Ort, IBAN, BIC, Gueltig ab, RT)
DocumentListener or ChangeListener fires on every field change and updates the corresponding ValueModel in PocModel via PocPresenter.bind()
Operator clicks 'Anordnen' button, triggering ActionListener in PocPresenter
PocPresenter.model.action() is called, iterating all 13 ModelProperties and collecting current ValueModel field values into a flat Map<String,String>
HttpBinService.post() serialises the Map to a flat JSON object via JSON-P streaming generator and sends HTTP POST to http://localhost:8080/post with Content-Type: application/json
HTTP response body is received and read; response code and body are logged to stdout
PocModel checks if response body is non-empty; if yes, emits the response body; if no, emits 'Failed operation'
EventEmitter.emit() notifies all registered EventListeners including the PocPresenter lambda
PocPresenter lambda sets textArea content to the event data, clears all 9 text fields to empty string, and resets gender to FEMALE

workflows.outcome: Form data submitted to backend, server response displayed in text area, all input fields cleared, gender reset to Female

workflows.name: Vue.js Person Search
workflows.description: The web client operator searches for a person in the mock dataset by entering one or more search criteria and clicking 'Suchen' to filter the results table.
workflows.trigger: User clicks the 'Suchen' (Search) button in Search.vue

steps.action: Operator enters one or more search values into the person form fields (last name, first name, ZIP, city, street, house number)
searchPerson() is invoked, resets search_result to an empty array
Iterates search_space (5 hardcoded persons), checking each person against all non-empty form field criteria using OR logic
For text fields: case-insensitive indexOf comparison; for ZIP: exact string equality
Matching person records are pushed into search_result array
Vue reactivity updates the results table to display matching records

workflows.outcome: Search results table populated with matching person records from mock dataset

workflows.name: Transfer to Allegro (Nach ALLEGRO uebernehmen)
workflows.description: After searching for a person and selecting both a person record and a Zahlungsempfaenger (payment recipient), the web operator transfers the combined data to the Allegro desktop application via WebSocket.
workflows.trigger: User clicks 'Nach ALLEGRO uebernehmen' button in Search.vue

steps.action: Operator has completed WF-002 (person search) and selected a person row, loading that person's Zahlungsempfaenger sub-table
Operator selects a Zahlungsempfaenger row from the second table (IBAN, BIC, Gueltig ab columns)
Operator clicks 'Nach ALLEGRO uebernehmen' button, invoking sendMessage(selected_result, 'textfield')
sendMessage() deep-clones the selected person object via JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(e))
The clone's zahlungsempfaenger array is replaced with the single zahlungsempfaenger_selected object
The payload { target: 'textfield', content: clonedPersonWithSingleZahlungsempfaenger } is JSON.stringify'd and sent via WebSocket to ws://localhost:1337
Node.js WebSocket server receives the UTF-8 message and broadcasts it to ALL connected clients
All WebSocket subscribers (including any connected Allegro desktop clients) receive the broadcast message

workflows.outcome: Selected person record with single chosen Zahlungsempfaenger broadcast to all WebSocket clients including Allegro desktop

workflows.name: Automatic Textarea WebSocket Broadcast
workflows.description: Any text typed into the textarea in the Vue.js Search component is automatically broadcast over WebSocket to all connected clients on every keystroke without requiring user button interaction.
workflows.trigger: User types or modifies text in the textarea within Search.vue

steps.action: Operator types in the textarea; Vue v-model updates internal_content_textarea data property
Vue watch on internal_content_textarea fires with the new value
sendMessage(val, 'textarea') is called automatically
Payload { target: 'textarea', content: val } is JSON.stringify'd and sent via WebSocket to ws://localhost:1337
Node.js server broadcasts the message to all connected clients

workflows.outcome: Textarea content is live-broadcast to all WebSocket clients on every change

workflows.name: WebSocket Connection Lifecycle (Vue Client)
workflows.description: The Search.vue component manages WebSocket connection to the Node.js server across its Vue lifecycle.

steps.action: Component mounted hook fires, calling connect()
connect() creates a new WebSocket to ws://localhost:1337
On socket open, status is set to 'connected'
Socket remains open for the lifetime of the component to relay all subsequent sendMessage() calls
disconnect() can optionally be called to close the socket and reset status (not triggered automatically)

workflows.outcome: Active WebSocket connection maintained for the Vue component lifetime enabling data transfer to Allegro

workflows.name: WebSocket Server Broadcast Relay
workflows.description: The Node.js server receives a message from any client and synchronously broadcasts it to all connected clients.
workflows.trigger: A WebSocket client sends a UTF-8 text message to the server

steps.action: message event fires on the server-side connection object with a message of type 'utf8'
The raw json string (message.utf8Data) is logged to stdout
Server iterates the clients array and calls sendUTF(json) on each client connection
All connected clients receive the message including the original sender

workflows.outcome: Message broadcast to all connected WebSocket clients without filtering or transformation

business_rules.name: Complete Form Submission Payload
business_rules.description: All 13 form fields (TEXT_AREA, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH, ZIP, ORT, STREET, IBAN, BIC, VALID_FROM, MALE, FEMALE, DIVERSE) must be collected and submitted together as a single flat JSON object on each action invocation.

business_rules.name: Null Field NullPointerException Risk
business_rules.description: All form field values are converted via Object.toString() before JSON serialisation. If any ValueModel contains null at the time action() is called, a NullPointerException will be thrown. The form should be fully populated before submission.

business_rules.name: HTTP POST Target Endpoint
business_rules.description: Form data is always submitted via HTTP POST to the hardcoded endpoint http://localhost:8080/post with Content-Type: application/json. This endpoint is not configurable in the current implementation.

business_rules.name: JSON Field Keys Match Enum Names
business_rules.description: The JSON object keys submitted in the HTTP POST body are the exact string representations of the ModelProperties enum constants (e.g. 'FIRST_NAME', 'IBAN', 'FEMALE'). This matches the property names defined in the OpenAPI specification's PostObject schema.

business_rules.name: Success Event on Non-Empty Response
business_rules.description: A non-empty HTTP response body from the POST request is treated as a successful submission. The full response body string is emitted as the event data to all EventEmitter subscribers.

business_rules.name: Failure Event on Empty Response
business_rules.description: An empty HTTP response body is treated as a failed submission. The literal string 'Failed operation' is emitted as the event data instead of the response body.

business_rules.name: Form Reset After Submission Event
business_rules.description: After receiving any event (success or failure) from the model layer, all 9 text input fields (firstName, name, dateOfBirth, zip, ort, street, iban, bic, validFrom) are cleared to empty string.

business_rules.name: Response Displayed in Text Area
business_rules.description: After receiving any event from the model layer, the full event data string (HTTP response body or 'Failed operation') is set as the content of the textArea component.

business_rules.name: Gender Reset to Female After Submission
business_rules.description: After receiving any event from the model layer, the gender radio buttons are explicitly reset: female.setSelected(true), male.setSelected(false), diverse.setSelected(false).

business_rules.name: Gender Mutual Exclusion (ButtonGroup)
business_rules.description: The three gender radio buttons (Weiblich/Female, Maennlich/Male, Divers/Diverse) are added to a single ButtonGroup, enforcing that only one can be selected at any time at the Swing UI level.

business_rules.name: Default Gender is Female
business_rules.description: When the form first loads, the gender radio button 'Weiblich' (Female) is pre-selected by calling female.setSelected(true) in initUI(). This is also the state restored after each form submission.

business_rules.name: Real-Time Text Field Synchronisation
business_rules.description: Every character inserted into or deleted from any of the 10 Swing text fields immediately updates the corresponding ValueModel via a DocumentListener. The complete current document text is read on each event using document.getText(0, document.getLength()).

business_rules.name: Real-Time Radio Button Synchronisation
business_rules.description: Every selection state change of any of the 3 gender radio buttons immediately updates the corresponding Boolean ValueModel by calling source.isSelected() inside a ChangeListener.

business_rules.name: 13 Form Fields Canonical Schema
business_rules.description: The ModelProperties enum defines exactly 13 form fields as the authoritative schema shared across all application layers. The complete set is: TEXT_AREA, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH, ZIP, ORT, STREET, IBAN, BIC, VALID_FROM, MALE, FEMALE, DIVERSE.

business_rules.name: All Fields Present in Model at Startup
business_rules.description: All 13 ModelProperties entries are initialised in PocModel's EnumMap with null-valued ValueModel instances at construction time, regardless of whether the user has entered any data. No field entry can be absent from the map.

business_rules.name: Application Stays Running Until Window Close
business_rules.description: Main.java uses CountDownLatch.await() to block the main thread indefinitely. The JFrame is configured with EXIT_ON_CLOSE. The application terminates only when the user closes the Swing window.

business_rules.name: MVP Construction Order Enforced
business_rules.description: PocView and EventEmitter must be constructed before PocModel (which depends on EventEmitter), and PocModel must be constructed before PocPresenter (which depends on both). This order is hard-wired in Main.java.

business_rules.name: OR-Combined Multi-Field Person Search
business_rules.description: Person search conditions across different fields are OR-combined. A person record matches if ANY of the filled-in search fields (last, first, zip, ort, street, hausnr) produces a match. Empty fields are skipped and do not filter out records.

business_rules.name: Case-Insensitive Substring Matching for Text Search Fields
business_rules.description: For last name, first name, city, street, and house number search fields, matching uses case-insensitive substring search: element.fieldName.toLowerCase().indexOf(formdata.field.toLowerCase()) >= 0.

business_rules.name: Exact String Equality for ZIP Code Search
business_rules.description: ZIP code (PLZ) search uses exact string equality (element.zip == formdata.zip) rather than substring matching, unlike all other text search fields.

business_rules.name: Client-Side Search Against Static Mock Dataset
business_rules.description: Person search executes entirely client-side against a static hardcoded in-memory dataset (search_space array). No HTTP or backend call is made during search. Results are immediately available without latency.

business_rules.name: Mock Dataset Contains Exactly 5 Persons
business_rules.description: The search_space dataset contains exactly 5 hardcoded person records: Hans Mayer (knr:79423984, 2 payment recipients), Linda Reitmayr (knr:67485246, 1 payment recipient), Karl May (knr:13725246, 3 payment recipients), Jens Mueller (knr:41125291, 2 payment recipients), Steffi Ruckmueller (knr:31228193, 2 payment recipients).

business_rules.name: Two-Step Selection: Person then Zahlungsempfaenger
business_rules.description: Transferring data to Allegro requires two sequential selection steps: (1) click a person row in the search results table to set selected_result, (2) click a Zahlungsempfaenger row in the sub-table to set zahlungsempfaenger_selected. Both must be done before clicking the transfer button.

business_rules.name: Zahlungsempfaenger Array Replaced with Single Selection
business_rules.description: When sending person data to Allegro with target='textfield', the full zahlungsempfaenger array on the cloned person object is replaced with only the single user-selected zahlungsempfaenger_selected entry before transmission.

business_rules.name: Deep Clone Before Mutation
business_rules.description: The person record object is deep-cloned via JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(e)) before modifying the zahlungsempfaenger field. This prevents mutation of the original search_space data that would corrupt subsequent searches.

business_rules.name: WebSocket Payload Structure
business_rules.description: All outgoing WebSocket messages from the Vue.js client conform to the structure { target: String, content: Object|String } serialised as a JSON string. The 'target' field identifies the intended destination component in the receiving Allegro desktop application.

business_rules.name: Target 'textfield' for Person Data
business_rules.description: When the user clicks 'Nach ALLEGRO uebernehmen', the sendMessage() call uses target='textfield' to indicate that the content should populate the form text fields in the Allegro desktop application.

business_rules.name: Target 'textarea' for Textarea Content
business_rules.description: Textarea content is transmitted with target='textarea', indicating that the content should be placed in the text area component of the receiving application rather than in individual form fields.

business_rules.name: Disabled Fields Are Read-Only and Non-Searchable
business_rules.description: Fields marked as disabled in the Vue.js search form (knr/Kundennummer, iban, bic, betriebsbez, rvnr, postfach, bgnr, vorsatzwort, leistung, traegernr) are read-only and cannot be used as search criteria. They are intended to be populated by the system after a search result is selected.

business_rules.name: Automatic Textarea WebSocket Broadcast on Every Change
business_rules.description: The Vue watch on internal_content_textarea fires on every change and automatically calls sendMessage(val, 'textarea'). This broadcasts the textarea content to all WebSocket subscribers on every keystroke without requiring any button click.

business_rules.name: Initial Textarea Content from App.vue Prop
business_rules.description: The Search component receives its initial textarea content ('Search Mock') from the App.vue parent via the content_textarea prop, which initialises internal_content_textarea in the component's data() function.

business_rules.name: WebSocket Connection Established on Component Mount
business_rules.description: The Vue.js Search component establishes its WebSocket connection to ws://localhost:1337 automatically in the mounted() lifecycle hook by calling connect(). This ensures the channel is ready before any user interaction.

business_rules.name: WebSocket Server Port 1337
business_rules.description: Both the Node.js WebSocket server (WebsocketServer.js) and the Vue.js client (Search.vue) use port 1337 on localhost as the agreed WebSocket connection endpoint.

business_rules.name: Unconditional Origin Acceptance
business_rules.description: The Node.js WebSocket server accepts connections from any origin unconditionally using request.accept(null, request.origin). No origin whitelist, authentication, or authorisation is applied.

business_rules.name: UTF-8 Only Message Processing
business_rules.description: The Node.js WebSocket server only processes and relays messages with type === 'utf8'. Binary messages are received but not processed (no else branch), effectively discarding them silently.

business_rules.name: Broadcast to All Clients Including Sender
business_rules.description: The Node.js server broadcasts every received message to ALL entries in the clients array, including the original sender. There is no filtering, exclusion of sender, or targeted routing.

business_rules.name: API Schema Gender Fields Typed as String
business_rules.description: In the OpenAPI 3.0.1 specification (api.yml), the FEMALE, MALE, and DIVERSE fields in the PostObject schema are typed as 'string', despite being stored as Boolean values in the Java PocModel. This is a type mismatch between the API contract and the implementation (Object.toString() on Boolean produces 'true' or 'false').

business_rules.name: Tyrus WebSocket Library Included But Not Yet Used
business_rules.description: The Maven pom.xml includes the GlassFish Tyrus standalone WebSocket client library as a dependency, suggesting that the Java Swing application is planned to also connect to the WebSocket server (ws://localhost:1337). However, no WebSocket client code exists in the current Java codebase; the Java client uses only HTTP via HttpBinService.

form_fields.name: IBAN
form_fields.note: Disabled in Vue form; comes from Zahlungsempfaenger selection

form_fields.name: BIC
form_fields.note: Disabled in Vue form; comes from Zahlungsempfaenger selection

form_fields.name: VALID_FROM
form_fields.note: Comes from Zahlungsempfaenger selection in Vue client

form_fields.name: FEMALE
form_fields.note: Default gender; Boolean in model, serialised as 'true'/'false' string in API

form_fields.name: MALE
form_fields.note: Mutually exclusive with FEMALE and DIVERSE

form_fields.name: DIVERSE
form_fields.note: Mutually exclusive with FEMALE and MALE

form_fields.name: TEXT_AREA
form_fields.note: Displays server response in Swing after submission; auto-broadcasts over WebSocket in Vue on every change

integration_points.name: HTTP POST to localhost:8080/post
integration_points.description: Java Swing client submits all 13 form fields as a flat JSON object to the echo endpoint. Defined in HttpBinService.java.

integration_points.name: WebSocket Broadcast Server
integration_points.direction: Bidirectional (Vue Client <-> Node.js Server <-> All Clients)
integration_points.description: Node.js server on port 1337 accepts WebSocket connections from any origin and broadcasts every received UTF-8 message to all connected clients. Vue.js client connects on mount and sends person transfer and textarea messages.

integration_points.name: Vue.js to Allegro Data Transfer via WebSocket
integration_points.direction: Vue Client -> Node.js Server -> Allegro Desktop (planned)
integration_points.description: Vue.js Search component sends person records (with single selected Zahlungsempfaenger) and textarea content as JSON payloads over WebSocket to the Node.js broadcast server, which relays to all subscribers.

payload_schema.content: Person record Object (for textfield) or String (for textarea)
